exemplar.title: To what extent have the economic sanctions changed life in Russia?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have the economic sanctions changed life in Russia?

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately with clear explanation of key concepts and a signposted structure
Research methodology draws on a reasonable range of secondary sources and published data
Research question is clearly stated and consistently referred to throughout the introduction
weaknesses: Research question remains broad with partial focus on social/political aspects
Methodological rationale is minimal and lacks discussion of source selection criteria or triangulation
Occasional digressions and minor lapses in wording reduce coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the topic is effectively communicated and key terms are defined, the research question is still broad and the methodology section lacks depth and justification. Two strands fit solidly into the 3â4 band (RQ focus and methodology), and only the communication strand reaches the 5â6 band. On balance, a holistic score of 4/6 reflects mostly effective performance with room to sharpen focus and expand methodological rationale.

strengths: Demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge of sanction evolution and economic indicators
Terminology and acronyms are mostly accurate and consistently used
Source material (statistics, timelines, tables) is applied in a generally relevant and appropriate manner
weaknesses: Occasional repetition and reportage reduce analytical depth
Minor inaccuracies in terminology (e.g., âeconomic channelsâ vs. âfinancial channelsâ)
Some data citations lack immediate verification or deeper interpretive linkage
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission shows solid factual knowledge, correct use of economic terms, and appropriate embedding of data. However, occasional slip-ups into description, minor inaccuracies, and incomplete integration of source material keep it in the middle of the 3â4 band. A score of 4/6 captures this consistently competent performance with modest room for deeper engagement.

strengths: Research draws on a fair breadth of sources that are generally relevant to the question
Some insightful analysis (e.g., black-market circumvention) enriches critical evaluation
The argument structure (negative vs. positive effects) provides a coherent framework
weaknesses: Analysis often remains explanatory without fully exploring alternative causal factors
Evaluation of arguments is thin and the conclusion largely restates findings rather than critically synthesising
Positive effects section is underdeveloped, limiting balanced discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work goes beyond description by linking evidence to effects, but many causal claims lack deeper modelling of alternatives and the evaluative component is incomplete. This aligns with the lower half of the 4â6 band for critical thinking. A holistic 4/12 reflects good relevance and emerging analysis but only partial critical evaluation and synthesis.

strengths: Logical progression with clear introduction, headings, table of contents and figure numbering
Layout (margins, spacing, fonts, numbering) generally supports readability
Effective use of diagrams to visualise policy milestones
weaknesses: Table of contents omits bibliography and some figure captions are missing or conflated with analysis
Section lengths and figure placements sometimes disrupt the flow of the narrative
Inconsistent formatting of captions and occasional OCR artefacts detract from polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure and layout meet the criteria for clear organisation, but inconsistencies in the contents page, figure captions, and flow interruptions prevent it from reaching the top band. A score of 3/4 reflects generally appropriate presentation with minor flaws.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay moves beyond description by linking currency data to automobile sales and oil-sector output, yet much of the writing remains explanatory rather than analytical. Cause-and-effect statements are sometimes asserted without modelling alternative factors, and final conclusions generalise (âsanctions have indeed had a huge impactâ) without measuring magnitude. Support is partial, so a low-to-mid score in the 4-6 band is appropriate.

Roughly thirty diverse sources, charts and official statistics demonstrate a fair breadth of research that stays largely on-task. However, reliance on media commentary and lack of primary research prevent a âconsistently excellentâ judgement. The work therefore sits mid-band (4-6), and a score of 5 reflects the stronger end of âgoodâ relevance.

The essay follows a logical progression: introduction, negative impacts, positive impacts, conclusion, and bibliography, aided by a contents page and figure numbering. Some sections are disproportionately long, figure placement disrupts flow, and sub-headings occasionally overlap, yet overall structure supports understanding. This aligns with the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects minor weaknesses.

The student demonstrates solid factual knowledge of the evolution of EU/G7 sanctions, rouble depreciation, and Russiaâs export redirection. Explanations are coherent but occasionally slip into reportage or repetition. Use of sources is partially effectiveâeconomic concepts are invoked but not always fully integratedâso the performance aligns with the 3-4 band.

A two-sided structure (negative vs. positive effects) creates some discussion, yet evaluation of competing arguments is thinâfor instance, humanitarian costs are listed but not weighed against strategic objectives. The conclusion restates rather than critically synthesises findings. This demonstrates âgood reasoned argument with partial critical evaluation,â meriting the lower half of the 4-6 band.

The paper shows page numbering, word count, headings, tables and images. Figure captions and footnotes are inconsistently formatted, and some OCR artefacts remain, but layout standards (margins, spacing, font consistency) are generally met. Thus layout considerations are âpresent and correctly appliedâ though not flawlessâhence a 3 within the upper band.

Terms such as depreciation, embargo, smart power, inflation and interest-rate hike are employed correctly, and acronyms (LNG, LNG, CAATSA) are defined. Minor inaccuracies (e.g., âeconomic channelsâ vs. âfinancial channelsâ) and occasional informal language reduce consistency, but overall usage is mostly accurate, fitting the middle band.

The topicâhow economic sanctions have affected life in Russiaâis stated in the heading, repeated word-for-word in the research question, and framed throughout the introduction. Key terms (economic sanctions, smart power, hard/soft power) are defined and contextualised, and the introduction signposts the structure to be followed. The explanation of sanctions and their intended purposes is detailed and generally accurate, giving the reader a clear sense of scope. Minor lapses in wording and occasional digressions prevent it from being fully âcomprehensive,â but overall communication is effective and accurate, meriting placement in the 5-6 band.

The student draws on public databases (Trading Economics, ExchangeRates.org, CREA, Consilium), think-tank reports, media outlets and UN bodies, indicating a reasonable range. All sources are secondary; no primary or interview data is attempted, and there is no explicit methodological rationale beyond âdata were collected.â Selection seems informedâgraphs are generated and referencedâbut some sources are journalistic or derivative, and no triangulation is explained. This places the work solidly in the âmostly completeâ category rather than the highest band.

The question is clear and placed prominently, but âto what extent have sanctions changed life in Russia?â remains broad, opening many economic, social and political sub-themes. The essay narrows itself mainly to macro-economic indicators and energy trade, showing only partial focus on âlifeâ (social well-being, political freedoms, etc. are treated briefly if at all). Connection between the RQ and later discussion is adequate, not tight; hence the strand fits the 3-4 band, at the upper end because the wording itself is unambiguous.

Statistics, timelines, survey results and exchange-rate data are embedded and generally support the narrative. Citations often lead to appropriate evidence (CFR, OHCHR, CREA), though some links are broken or duplicated, and a few claims (e.g., exact death tolls) are left without immediate verification. Overall application is mostly relevant and appropriate, earning the middle bandâs upper level.

annotations.comment: The bullet list of sanctions measures is clear and neatly organized, aiding comprehension of key EU responses.
Analysis of currency depreciation should consider alternative factors (e.g., oil price volatility) to avoid causal oversimplification.
Listing refinery outputs in bullet form offers clarity on product ranges, aiding comprehension of sectorâspecific impacts.
The discussion of interest rate hikes would benefit from comparison with pre-sanction rates to contextualize severity.
The âpositive effectsâ section is empty. Include concrete examples of adaptation or resilience among Russian citizens to provide balance.
The black-market analysis insightfully highlights unintended sanction circumvention, enriching the critical evaluation of outcomes.
The CREA bar graph lacks methodological context. Briefly explain data sources and selection criteria to justify its use.
The conclusion restates findings descriptively. Synthesize and evaluate whether sanctions achieved their strategic aims, linking back to the research question.
The research question remains very broad. Consider specifying which aspects of âlife in Russiaâ (e.g., social welfare, political freedoms) you will assess to sharpen the focus.
The table of contents omits the bibliography. Including all major sections ensures the reader can navigate to references efficiently.
The introduction provides solid definitions but lacks the studentâs interpretation of these concepts. Adding brief analytical comments would demonstrate deeper understanding.
The figure caption conflates description and argument. Separate the caption from analytical text in the body to enhance readability.
Figure 3 appears without a clear caption immediately above it. Adding a descriptive caption directly above or below improves presentation.
The cited death toll of 27 449 civilians is presented without linkage to life-quality impacts. Discuss how this statistic relates to social well-being in Russia.
The section on the referendum recounts events descriptively but lacks critique of source bias. Evaluate reliability of each account to strengthen analysis.
The text uses an incorrect math symbol âÎµ300 billionâ instead of the euro sign (â¬ 300 billion). Use the proper currency symbol for clarity.
The chronological diagram effectively visualizes sanction milestones, strengthening understanding of policy evolution.
Listing negative effects without integrating them into the narrative can fragment analysis. Tie each list item directly to supporting evidence.
Each listed disadvantage should be accompanied by a citation or data point to substantiate the claim.
The line graph provides insightful data, but the analysis should explicitly reference axis labels and units to clarify the trend discussion.
The data table of USD/RUB rates is well-structured, providing a clear quantitative basis for currency analysis.
The description of the âlaw of supplyâ contradicts observed trends. Revisit economic definitions to accurately frame the anomaly.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (italics, punctuation). Standardize citation style (e.g., Harvard) across all references.
The title is concise and directly reflects the investigationâs focus, establishing clear alignment between the title and research question.
The student correctly provides a word count within the 4 000-word limit, demonstrating adherence to formatting requirements.
The abbreviations list is helpful, but the newly introduced acronym âCAATSAâ should be included here for consistency.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (e.g., use of <sup> tags). Standardize footnote or in-text citation style to improve presentation clarity.
Footnotes display OCR artifacts (e.g., âlt;sup>â). Revise footnote formatting to eliminate encoding errors.
The USAID footnote uses a math tag for the superscript. Remove the math environment to maintain consistent footnote styling.
The fraction \(\frac{2}{3}\) appears as a math tag in running text. Integrate the statistic into prose or footnote rather than display math.
